data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures advance as Trump's chip tariffs come with broad carve outs
data.description:
Stock index futures gained on Thursday, as traders weighed President Donald Trump's latest chip tariffs, softened by sweeping exemptions.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.6%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.8%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.5%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.24%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.72%.

Trump said Wednesday he plans to impose a 100% tariff on semiconductor imports, with an exemption for companies that manufacture or commit to manufacturing in the U.S.

Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) clinched a record high on Thursday after Taiwan said its top chip manufacturer will not have to pay a 100% tariff on such imports to the U.S.

"U.S. manufacturers live in confusing times. Alongside the widespread increases in taxes on imports that took effect this morning, Trump suggested a 100% tax on imports of microchips. However, in a maneuver that was less a retreat and more a pirouette, these taxes will not apply to imports from some specific companies, including some of the largest exporters to the U.S.," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

On the economic side, the initial jobless claims jumped more than expected and the continuing claims rose to the highest mark since 2021

Labor cost growth cooled markedly in Q2, while productivity rose more than expected.

In more tariff news, Trump hiked the tariff on Indian goods to 50% from 25%, citing India's continued importation of Russian oil.

U.S. stocks rebounded and closed higher on Wednesday, helped by a surge in Apple (AAPL). Traders waded through a slew of quarterly reports from several big companies.

"Tech stocks have continued to drive a buoyant mood in markets, with the Mag-7 reaching a new all-time high. Momentum was also supported by strong earnings and rising hopes of imminent rate cuts as Federal Reserve speakers turned more dovish in response to last Friday’s weak payroll print," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

The upbeat tone has continued overnight despite Trump outlining a plan for 100% tariffs on semiconductors, with the impact of these mitigated by carveouts, Sidorov added.

The June Wholesale Inventories report will land during market hours. No revision is expected in the revised figure from an increase of 0.2 percent in the flash.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Cronos misses Q2 top-line and bottom-line estimates
Labor cost growth cools markedly in Q2, productivity rises more than expected
data.description: Nonfarm labor productivity increased 2.4% Q/Q in Q2, faster than the +1.9% expected and accelerating -1.8% in Q1 (revised from -1.5%), according to data released by the U.S. Department of Labor on Thursday.

data.title: Jazz Pharma wins FDA nod for Modeyso against rare brain tumor
data.description:
• Jazz Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:JAZZ) has received the U.S. FDA's approval for Modeyso (dordaviprone), an orally administered once-weekly therapy for a rare brain tumor known as diffuse midline glioma, as a late-line option.
  • Specifically, the agency has granted accelerated approval for Modeyso therapy following previous treatment for those aged at least a year with progressive diffuse midline glioma harboring the H3 K27M mutation.
  • Continuous approval for the drug against the indication will be subject to data from the company's Phase 3 ACTION confirmatory trial, which is currently underway, with a primary completion date in August 2026.
  • The approval was backed by data from five open-label clinical studies, in which 50 patients with recurrent H3K27M-mutant diffuse midline glioma indicated an overall response rate of 22% after receiving Modeyso.

data.title: Addus HomeCare names Heather Dixon as Chief Operating Officer
data.description:
• Addus HomeCare (NASDAQ:ADUS) Thursday named Heather Dixon as President and Chief Operating Officer.
  • Dixon is expected to join the company in that role in mid-September.
  • W. Bradley Bickham, the company’s current President and Chief Operating Officer, has been appointed advisor to the Chief Executive Officer upon Dixon joining and has agreed to serve in that role until his previously announced retirement in March 2026.
